tx.en: Use your imagination to feel strong

story.en: If you ever feel lonely, I got your back. Use your imagination! Pretend your plushies or imaginary friends are talking and playing with you. Your mind can create company whenever you need it.

tx.en: Breathe in friendship... breathe out lonely feelings

q.en: What can you do if you feel lonely?

tx.en: Your mind is a great friend
You don't need to ask to belong

story.en: To play with other kids, you don't always have to ask their names. Just go where they are playing. When time passes, they will just accept you. It's how kids play!

q.en: What is a simple way to join a game?

op: Just be where they are playing
ex: Joining the space leads to playing
Too many questions stop the fun

story.en: If you have a headache in class, don't look at the screen or the teacher. Just hear her voice. Memorize the answers in your head so you don't have to use your eyes. Your ears can learn too!

q.en: How do you learn if your head hurts from the light?

tx.en: Rest your eyes, use your mind

inf.en: Keeps you in the lesson safely

tx.en: Balance your fun and your work

story.en: To be the smartest, play for 5 to 20 minutes only. Don't play too much so you don't get distracted. Then, review your homework. Balance is the secret of a god.

q.en: How long should you play to stay focused?

ex: All day makes you lose focus
Short play keeps the brain fresh

story.en: If you are lazy, you get an F. But if you work like a god, you get an A. Don't think one A is enough; you need more and more to be the smartest in your class. Keep going!

q.en: What happens when you work like a god?

tx.en: I am the smartest in my class

story.en: When you are with your family or adults you trust, observe everything they do. Watch their encounters and actions. Being alert makes you smart and keeps you safe in any place.

q.en: Why should you observe adults you trust?

tx.en: Be still and watch the world

story.en: Even in a parking zone, stay alert. Look at the movement of other cars around you. Never stop paying attention until you are safely inside. You are the master of your own safety.

tx.en: One last scan of the area

q.en: When should you stop being attentive?

ex: Seeing the car is just the start
Attention must stay until you are safe

tx.en: I am safe. I am alert. I am ready.
